name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years of analytical method development, multi-technique characterization, and DOE/SPC process-development experience across small-molecule, polymer, thin-film, and functional materials systems. Currently leads bio-based polymer process-development work at CU Boulder, building reproducible analytical datasets, stability/degradation readouts, SOP-ready documentation, and technical reports that support development decisions.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided polymer process-development experiments, defining critical process parameters, analytical readouts, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready controls for scale-up decisions.

Develop stability/degradation and release-relevant characterization readouts using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR-adjacent spectroscopy, thermal analysis, and SPC concepts to connect process conditions with material quality attributes.
Translate process and characterization data into technical reports, troubleshooting recommendations, and documented methods suitable for cross-functional development review.

Designed and characterized 15+ organic electrochromic material variants using UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, NMR, GPC, TGA, DSC, and mass-spectrometry-informed analysis.
Assessed structure-property-stability relationships, degradation pathways, impurity-/failure-mode hypotheses, and method comparability to guide molecular and formulation/process decisions.
Built rigorous experimental documentation and datasets supporting method development, reproducibility assessment, and root-cause analysis.
Co-led AFOSR-funded molecular thin-film process development, linking CVD/surface preparation with morphology, interfaces, optical performance, and reproducible process controls.
Prepared technical data packages connecting process variables, material performance, and risk/quality decisions for multidisciplinary stakeholders.
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, characterizing film-growth kinetics to improve coating reproducibility.
Managed operations for a 15-member process-development laboratory, implementing equipment tracking, calibration routines, SOPs, and safety protocols that improved instrument uptime by 30%.
Trained researchers on instrument workflows, experimental documentation, and safe handling while coordinating multiple concurrent materials-development projects.

projects.title: Analytical Method Development and Stability Study
projects.description: Mapped degradation, redox-instability, and performance failure modes across functional material variants using orthogonal analytical characterization and documented method comparisons.
projects.tech: UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, CV, NMR, GPC, TGA/DSC, MS-informed analysis

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Scale-Up Process
projects.description: Defined DOE-guided process windows, analytical readouts, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready specifications for scalable polymer systems.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, thermal analysis, technical reports

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Thin-Film Coatings
projects.description: Connected CVD and surface-preparation variables to coating morphology, interfaces, and optical/material performance in thin-film systems.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Method Development & Stability: Analytical readouts, degradation studies, impurity/failure-mode hypotheses, reproducibility studies, method documentation, SOP-ready controls
skills.Process & Quality Mindset: DOE, SPC, process windows, troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, technical reports, validation-package adjacency, tech-transfer documentation
skills.Data & Communication: Experimental design, data visualization, Excel, Python/basic data analysis, JMP/basic, cross-functional technical presentations

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
